text:
In a small town nestled between whispering pines, lived a girl named Clara. She had big blue eyes that always seemed to look right through you, a knack for finding abandoned things, and an uncanny ability to remember dreams. Every summer, she discovered something different under an overgrown apple tree in the woods behind her house.

This particular summer she found an ancient-looking journal. It was filled with sketches that resembled winged cherubs and notes that seemed to discuss forbidden knowledge and ancient forests. Something about reading its last entry made her feel strangely curious.

She copied down the entries, which included how to find a magical compass that pointed to the heart of an enchanted forest. They spoke of wisdom and secret creatures. Clara got toiling in her own little library of dream findings.

One night, she dreamt she was standing beside a river, its waters running amber. A small, fox-like creature popped out of the water. She learned it could only be awakened by laughing. And she laughed
